course.title: Verifiable Randomness & Cryptographic Time
course.tagline: Build and break biased lotteries — then verify proofs offline from 80 bytes.

m1.title: Chaos VRF with Platon
m1.concept: Unbiasable randomness from a verifiable chaos oracle.

m2.title: VDF time with Chronos
m2.concept: Vesolovsky VDF proves elapsed work — not wall-clock trust.

m3.title: ECVRF & Sortes
m3.concept: RFC 9381 ECVRF proofs you can verify offline.

m4.title: Time-lock RSW with Aestus
m4.concept: Encrypt now, decrypt after provable delay.

m5.title: Commit–reveal & bias attacks
m5.concept: Build a biased lottery, then break it with verifiable draws.

m6.title: On-chain verification
m6.concept: Verify VDF and VRF proofs in Solidity on Base testnet.

site.quickstart_intro: Clone the repo, install oracle deps, run labs locally or in Colab.
site.exercises_body: Pass all checks with python labs/run_exercises.py.
site.certificate_intro: Complete labs and exercises to unlock your certificate.
site.certificate_locked: Check off all labs and exercises to unlock
site.footer: Part of the AIMarket ecosystem — Platon, Chronos, Sortes, and Aestus oracles as a live sandbox.

exercises.heading: Your turn
exercises.lab01_hint: Verify a Platon draw with your own client_seed. Check: python labs/run_exercises.py --module m1
exercises.lab02_hint: Tamper a VDF output and watch verify fail. Check: python labs/run_exercises.py --module m2
exercises.lab03_hint: Confirm the ECVRF proof is exactly 80 bytes. Check: python labs/run_exercises.py --module m3
exercises.lab04_hint: Seal and open a message with T=300. Check: python labs/run_exercises.py --module m4
exercises.lab05_hint: Compare biased vs fair lottery tickets. Check: python labs/run_exercises.py --module m5
exercises.lab06_hint: Build an on-chain VDF vector. Check: python labs/run_exercises.py --module m6

lab05.audit_note: Fair draw binds one beta per seed — operator cannot grind.

lab06.deploy_note: See lottery/docs/deployments-base.md for ChronosVDF on Base.
